Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 238. The Obstinate Qappatean (ABL 0980)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-ia

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Taklak-ana-Bel. Good health to the king, my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka mtak-lak—a-na—EN

o 33

lu šul-mu a-na LUGAL EN-ía

o 44

ina UGU URU.qa-pa-ta-a.a

(4) As to the Qappatean about whom the king, my lord, wrote to me: "Who is this man? Did the town perhaps persuade him not to c[ome ...] to Dur-[Šarruke]n? [......

o 55

ša LUGAL be-lí iš-pur-an-[ni*]

o 66

ma-a LÚ : man-nu šu-u-tú

o 77

⸢ma*⸣-[a] ina sur-re-e URU : ú-ša-ad-bi-bu-⸢šú*⸣

o 88

ma a-na URU.⸢BÀD*⸣—[mMAN—GI].⸢NA⸣ [x x]

o 99

⸢la⸣-a ⸢tal*⸣-[x x x x x]

o 1010

LÚv*.⸢qur*⸣-[bu-te x x x x x x]

o 1111

⸢ma*⸣-a* [x x x x x x]

o 1212

⸢LÚv*⸣.[x x x x x x x]

rest broken away

(Break)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

an-nu-⸢rig⸣ [x x x x]

(r 1) I am right now [...] sendi[ng him] to the king, [my lord].

r 2'2'

ina UGU LUGAL [EN-ia]

r 3'3'

ú-se-bi-[la-šú]

rest (10 lines) blank

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334658/.
